_metadata._verificationNotes: Core curriculum structures and general academic features are broadly accurate based on public university catalog information. However, specific course examples, faculty names, and some program details may be approximate or outdated. Users should consult individual university catalogs for current course offerings.

coreRequirements.description: General Education Program requiring courses across 4 categories. No single required core curriculum; instead, students must fulfill distribution requirements across academic areas.
coreRequirements.foreignLanguageRequirement: Citation-level proficiency or 4 semesters of study
coreRequirements.thesisRequirement: None required for bachelor's; optional in most concentrations for honors

Economics.seniorThesis: Optional but strongly recommended for honors track
Economics.typicalTimeline: Declare sophomore fall, courses must be completed by senior spring
Economics.insiderTip: Ec 10 is the largest class at Harvard (500+ students). Getting into TF sections and building relationships with teaching fellows matters more than the professor.

Computer Science.typicalTimeline: Take CS 50 freshman year, progress to 100-level by sophomore
Computer Science.insiderTip: CS concentration is highly competitive. Internships at top tech companies (Google, Meta, Apple) during summers significantly boost career outcomes.

Applied Mathematics.typicalTimeline: Start with Math 21 or 55b sophomore year
Applied Mathematics.insiderTip: Applied Math students often double-concentrate with Physics or CS. The flexibility allows for cross-disciplinary research opportunities.

Biology.insiderTip: Harvard's Museum of Comparative Zoology and research centers provide unparalleled lab opportunities. Thesis work often becomes publications.

prerequisites: Two science courses at 50-level or above
History & Science.seniorThesis: Optional but common for interdisciplinary honors
History & Science.typicalTimeline: Can declare sophomore or junior year
History & Science.insiderTip: Unique Harvard concentration. Strong for pre-med students interested in medical history and policy.

Philosophy.insiderTip: Michael Sandel's 'Justice' (Phil 50) is legendary—one of Harvard's most popular courses. Philosophy pairs well with Law, Government, or Economics.

Harvard University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), plus pass/fail option for up to 4 courses per concentration
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Junior Tutorial system: 2-3 students work directly with a faculty member weekly
Senior thesis requirement in many concentrations, resulting in original research
Cross-registration with MIT (students can take up to 4 courses at MIT)
Expos 20 freshman writing seminar capped at 12 students
Open curriculum: no distribution requirements beyond gen-ed areas
Harvard University.researchOpportunities: PRISE (Physical Sciences Research Initiative for Summer Education), BLISS, 450+ research centers including Harvard Kennedy School, Wyss Institute, Black Hole Initiative
Harvard University.studyAbroad: ~25% of undergraduates study abroad. Programs in 40+ countries through Harvard-sponsored and approved programs. Semesters in London, Rome, Beijing, Berlin popular.

coreRequirements.description: Yale College offers flexibility through residential college system with no strict core curriculum. Distribution requirements across breadth of knowledge.
coreRequirements.writingRequirement: Writing courses throughout curriculum; residential college writing support
coreRequirements.foreignLanguageRequirement: Completion of Level 3 course or demonstrated proficiency

Economics.insiderTip: Yale's residential college system means you'll build close relationships with economics faculty and peers in your college.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Yale's undergraduate CS program emphasizes fundamentals and breadth. Internships at hedge funds (many in CT) highly lucrative.

Engineering Sciences.seniorThesis: Senior research project or thesis common
Engineering Sciences.insiderTip: Yale Engineering is smaller and more focused than larger programs, allowing for mentorship and collaboration.

Molecular Biophysics & Biochemistry.seniorThesis: Strongly recommended; leads to research publications
Molecular Biophysics & Biochemistry.insiderTip: Yale's 450+ acres includes research labs and medical school. Direct pathway to Yale Medical School research.

Physics.insiderTip: Yale has one of the strongest physics departments in the country. Undergraduates participate in CERN research.

uniqueAcademicFeatures: Residential college system: students live with same cohort for 4 years, creating tight communities
Master and Dean in each residential college provide mentorship and intellectual engagement
Flexible curriculum with no core requirements beyond distributions
Direct access to graduate course libraries and Yale's 15 libraries
Undergraduate seminars limited to 20 students
Yale University.researchOpportunities: URECA (Undergraduate Research), Summer Fellowship, 350+ faculty willing to mentor research, Yale Peabody Museum, Yale School of Medicine research programs
Yale University.studyAbroad: ~30% participate. Yale's program in Beijing, London, Paris, Jackson Hole environmental science program, and approved exchanges.
notableFaculty: Wendy Chun (Computer Science & Theory of Mind)
Mary Lui (American History, Asian American Studies)

coreRequirements.description: Flexible approach with general education requirements spread across first two years. Focus on breadth before specialization.
coreRequirements.writingRequirement: Writing seminars and writing-intensive courses throughout
coreRequirements.quantitativeRequirement: Two quantitative courses required in first two years

Engineering.seniorThesis: Junior year independent project + senior thesis required
Engineering.insiderTip: Princeton Engineering is one of the top programs. The Senior Thesis Project requirement produces publishable research.

Economics.seniorThesis: Optional senior thesis for honors students
Economics.insiderTip: Princeton's economics program is heavily theory-focused. Proof-based problem sets are rigorous and prepare for graduate study.

Physics.typicalTimeline: Core courses complete by senior year
Physics.insiderTip: Princeton's physics building is a hub for innovation. Undergraduate research leading to publication is common.

Computer Science.seniorThesis: Junior year independent work + senior thesis common
Computer Science.insiderTip: COS 126 is notoriously challenging. Success here correlates with strong career outcomes in tech.

Molecular Biology.seniorThesis: Senior thesis required; often leads to publication
Molecular Biology.insiderTip: Princeton's molecular biology program is elite. Undergraduates conduct publishable research under renowned faculty.

Princeton University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), can be taken pass/fail under certain conditions
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Flexible curriculum with strong emphasis on independent research
Junior Independent Work: required year-long research/creative project
Senior Thesis: required capstone project, often publishable quality
Precept system: small discussion sections with graduate students or faculty for large lectures
Close faculty-student interaction: 6:1 student-to-faculty ratio
Princeton University.researchOpportunities: Undergraduate summer research fellowships, over 100 faculty research groups, PIRE (Princeton Institute for Research in Economics), Center for Advanced Study, internships at Bell Labs and other research institutes
Princeton University.studyAbroad: ~40% study abroad. Semester programs in Berlin, Oxford, Santiago, Tokyo, and other locations. Also summer research abroad programs.
notableFaculty: Eric Maskin (Game Theory, Economics, Nobel laureate)

coreRequirements.description: Rigorous General Education Requirement for all Columbia College students. Structured core curriculum covering classics, history, literature, sciences.
coreRequirements.foreignLanguageRequirement: Reading proficiency in one language or 4 semesters
coreRequirements.historicalContextRequirement: Global Core courses exploring world civilizations

Economics.seniorThesis: Senior thesis optional; honors track encouraged
Economics.insiderTip: Columbia's location in Manhattan gives economics students access to Fed, Treasury, hedge funds, investment banks for internships.

coreCourses: COMS W3136 (Design of Usable Interactive Systems)
Computer Science.seniorThesis: Capstone project or senior thesis available
Computer Science.insiderTip: Columbia CS graduates dominate tech companies. Strong internship culture with Google, Meta, Amazon offices nearby.

coreCourses: Applied Math W4001 (Advanced Calculus & Partial Differential Equations)
Applied Mathematics.seniorThesis: Thesis work common for honors students
Applied Mathematics.typicalTimeline: Full math sequence through sophomore year
Applied Mathematics.insiderTip: Applied Math at Columbia is flexible and bridges to finance careers. Many students apply coursework to Wall Street internships.

Chemistry.seniorThesis: Senior thesis available; lab-based honors track
Chemistry.insiderTip: Columbia's location near pharmaceutical and biotech companies provides unmatched internship opportunities.

Political Science.insiderTip: Columbia's location in NYC creates opportunities for government internships and policy work. Many students go to grad school or work in think tanks.

Columbia University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/fail option for electives only (not core courses)
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Rigorous, mandatory General Education Core for all students—shared intellectual foundation
University Writing Seminar capped at 12 students in freshman year
Great Books seminars with famous authors and thinkers conducting readings
Preceptorial system: small sections paired with large lectures
Location in Manhattan: direct access to museums, libraries, government agencies, financial institutions
Columbia University.researchOpportunities: Columbia's Research Scholars Program, 300+ research centers including Earth Institute, Zuckerman Institute, SIPA, medical school research programs
Columbia University.studyAbroad: ~35% study abroad. Programs in 30+ countries. Popular: Oxford, London, Paris, Berlin, Beijing, Buenos Aires, Santiago.
notableFaculty: Gayatri Chakravorty Spivak (Literary Theory, Postcolonial Studies)

coreRequirements.description: Flexible general education with distribution requirements across six schools. One common set of requirements across engineering, business, arts & sciences.
coreRequirements.writingRequirement: Across curriculum; writing center support available
coreRequirements.foreignLanguageRequirement: Three semesters of study or equivalent proficiency

Economics (College of Arts & Sciences).typicalTimeline: Declare sophomore year, complete by senior spring
Economics (College of Arts & Sciences).insiderTip: UPenn Econ degree pairs well with Wharton exposure. Many students take electives across schools. Strong outcomes to finance, consulting, tech.

Engineering.insiderTip: UPenn Engineering is practical and project-based. Senior Design team competitions award internships and post-graduation offers.

Computer Science.insiderTip: UPenn CS is practical. Many students doing internships at Google, Meta, Apple, Microsoft. Cross-registration with Wharton common.

Biology.insiderTip: UPenn pre-meds dominate. Many students do research at Penn Medical School or Children's Hospital.

Chemistry.insiderTip: Unique strength in materials chemistry and nanotechnology. Undergraduate research leading to publications is common.

University of Pennsylvania.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus summer sessions)
University of Pennsylvania.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/fail option available for limited courses
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Four undergraduate schools: College of Arts & Sciences, School of Engineering & Applied Science, Wharton Business School, School of Nursing
Cross-school registration: can take classes at any school
Double degrees: CAS/Wharton dual degree popular (5-year program)
Hands-on learning: senior design projects, research labs, internship-based learning
Location in Philadelphia: museums, cultural institutions, tech startups, government agencies
University of Pennsylvania.researchOpportunities: Penn Summer Research Program, 300+ research centers including Penn Medicine research labs, Nano/Bio Interface Center, Penn Engineering research
University of Pennsylvania.studyAbroad: ~30% study abroad. Programs in London, Paris, Berlin, Beijing, Mumbai, Buenos Aires, and other cities.

coreRequirements.description: No formal core curriculum. Instead, Brown offers the Open Curriculum: students design their own path with guidance from advisors. Must complete Sc.B. requirements if doing science.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Brown CS is flexible and emphasizes breadth. Many students combine with applied math, neuroscience, or economics.

Applied Mathematics.insiderTip: Brown's Applied Math program is flexible and draws students from engineering, physics, computer science.

Biology.seniorThesis: Thesis common for honors; research leading to publication
Biology.insiderTip: Brown's open curriculum attracts biology students who want flexibility. Many combine with neuroscience, computer science, or philosophy.

Physics.seniorThesis: Thesis work common in honors track
Physics.insiderTip: Brown's physics program is strong in cosmology and astrophysics. Access to Brown's Laser Lab and research groups.

Neuroscience.insiderTip: Brown is famous for neuroscience research. Undergraduates work in labs at Brown Hospital and research institutes. Many go to top graduate programs.

Brown University.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus summer sessions)
Brown University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), satisfactory/no credit option (no GPA impact for satisfactory grades)
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Open Curriculum: no core requirements, students design education based on interests
Satisfactory/No Credit option: allows risk-taking without GPA penalty
Undergraduate teaching assistants: students become TAs as part of academic journey
Undergraduate research program: formal UTRA (Undergraduate Teaching & Research Awards)
Brown Medical School: research opportunities at affiliated hospital and medical campus
Brown University.researchOpportunities: UTRA (Undergraduate Teaching & Research Awards), Summer Fellowship, 400+ research labs including Brown Hospital, neuroscience institutes, engineering labs
Brown University.studyAbroad: ~35% study abroad. Programs in London, Paris, Barcelona, Berlin, Beijing, Buenos Aires, India, Indonesia.

coreRequirements.description: No formal core curriculum. Dartmouth's distribution system requires breadth across five groups but students have freedom to choose courses.
coreRequirements.quantitativeRequirement: Two math/science courses from the distribution

Computer Science.insiderTip: Dartmouth CS students are known for strong leadership and startup culture. Many alumni founded companies like Siri, Kayak.

Engineering.insiderTip: Dartmouth Engineering attracts entrepreneurial students. Small program (40-50 undergrads per class) means close mentorship.

Biology.insiderTip: Dartmouth's location enables field biology. Summer research in the mountains and at field stations.

Chemistry.insiderTip: Dartmouth chemistry has strong connection to pharmaceutical industry. Many students intern at nearby biotech companies.

Economics.insiderTip: Dartmouth Economics is small and intimate. Strong alumni network on Wall Street and in tech. Many go to law or graduate school.

Dartmouth College.academicCalendar: Unique quarter system (Fall, Winter, Spring, Summer). Each quarter is 10 weeks; students take 3-4 courses per quarter.
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Quarter system (not semester): four 10-week terms allow flexibility and exploration
Freshman outdoor orientation: all new students participate in outdoor trip before classes start
Tight-knit community: easy access to faculty, active undergraduate research culture
Green (the central campus): iconic gathering space, 250-acre green
Strong emphasis on undergraduate teaching: faculty prioritize teaching over research
Dartmouth College.researchOpportunities: Undergraduate research funding through grants, Summer Fellowship, Engineering Research Program, Environmental Studies field programs, Arctic field research, Hood Museum research
Dartmouth College.studyAbroad: ~30% study abroad. Programs in London, Paris, Berlin, Argentina, Costa Rica, East Africa, India, China.
notableFaculty: Dr. Chara Mowafi (Public Health, Global Health)

coreRequirements.description: Varies by college. College of Engineering requires engineering fundamentals; College of Arts & Sciences has distribution requirements. School of Applied Economics has specific business requirements.
coreRequirements.writingRequirement: Writing courses and writing support throughout curriculum

Engineering.insiderTip: Cornell Engineering is one of the largest and most respected. Strong industry partnerships and internship opportunities.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Cornell CS is rigorous and theoretical. Strong outcomes to top tech companies and graduate school.

Applied Economics & Management.insiderTip: AEM is unique in agricultural and rural economics. Many students go to venture capital, consulting, or finance.

Physics.insiderTip: Cornell has strong physics tradition and research opportunities. Access to Cornell's synchrotron research facility.

Biology.insiderTip: Cornell's location on Cayuga Lake enables exceptional field biology and ecology research.

Cornell University.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus summer)
Cornell University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), S/U (satisfactory/unsatisfactory) option for limited courses
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Multiple colleges within university: each has distinct culture and requirements
College of Engineering is one of the largest and most respected in US
Land-grant mission: strong emphasis on research and practical application
Gorges and waterfalls: scenic location with natural outdoor laboratory for field research
Strong STEM focus with excellent liberal arts offerings
Cornell University.researchOpportunities: Cornell Research: 300+ research centers, undergraduate research funding, summer research fellowships, engineering project teams, agriculture and environmental field research
Cornell University.studyAbroad: ~25% study abroad. Programs in London, Paris, Rome, Geneva, Beijing, Costa Rica, Australia.

coreRequirements.description: Stanford has flexible general education requirements with emphasis on breadth. No single core curriculum; instead, students fulfill distribution requirements in eight areas.
coreRequirements.writingRequirement: PWR (Program in Writing & Rhetoric) for all first-year students
coreRequirements.foreignLanguageRequirement: One course in foreign language or sign language

coreCourses: CS 161 (Design of Usable & Secure Interactive Systems)
Computer Science.seniorThesis: Honors thesis or senior capstone project
Computer Science.insiderTip: Stanford CS is the epicenter of tech entrepreneurship. Many students start companies while still in school. Internships at Google, Meta, Apple, etc. are standard.

Engineering.insiderTip: Stanford's close proximity to Silicon Valley enables internships and post-graduation opportunities in tech. Project-based learning is strong.

coreCourses: ECON 50 (Using Big Data to Solve Economic & Social Problems)
Economics.insiderTip: Stanford Economics emphasizes data analysis and technology. Strong outcomes to venture capital, tech, consulting, government.

Physics.seniorThesis: Senior thesis common in honors track
Physics.insiderTip: Stanford Physics has strong tradition in particle physics. Direct connections to SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory.

Symbolic Systems.typicalTimeline: Intro courses throughout; culminates senior year
Symbolic Systems.insiderTip: Unique Stanford interdisciplinary major. Attracts students interested in AI, cognitive science, and human-machine interaction.

Stanford University.academicCalendar: Quarter system (Fall, Winter, Spring, Summer). Each quarter is ~10 weeks; students take 3-5 courses per quarter.
Stanford University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/no pass option for limited courses
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Quarter system: four 10-week terms, more flexibility, can explore more topics
Proximity to Silicon Valley: unmatched access to tech industry, internships, startups
D-School (Design School): focus on design thinking and human-centered problem solving
Mayfield Fellows Program: business and entrepreneurship focus
Cardinal Quarter: optional fall quarter for first-years before official enrollment; bonding experience
Stanford University.researchOpportunities: Stanford Undergraduate Research Fellowship (SURF), 300+ research labs, Summer Research Internship Program, access to SLAC National Lab and LIGO gravitational wave detector
Stanford University.studyAbroad: ~20% study abroad. Programs in Berlin, Buenos Aires, Beijing, Cape Town, Florence, London, Madrid, Moscow.
notableFaculty: Andrew Ng (Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning—co-founder Coursera)

coreRequirements.description: All MIT students (regardless of major) must complete General Institute Requirements (GIRs) and departmental requirements. Strong emphasis on science and math fundamentals.

Computer Science & Engineering (6).seniorThesis: No formal thesis; but capstone projects common
Computer Science & Engineering (6).typicalTimeline: 6.0001 freshman, core courses sophomore/junior, electives throughout
Computer Science & Engineering (6).insiderTip: MIT 6.0001 and 6.1210 are notoriously hard but build legendary problem-solving skills. Many students intern at Google, Meta, Apple, Dropbox, etc.

Electrical Engineering & Computer Scienc.insiderTip: MIT EE is the gold standard in electrical engineering. Access to MIT Lincoln Laboratory research. Many post-graduation opportunities in semiconductors and power.

Physics.insiderTip: MIT Physics is elite and rigorous. Access to MIT's particle detectors and research at Brookhaven National Lab.

Chemistry.insiderTip: MIT Chemistry leads to top pharma, materials, or chemical companies. Strong industry recruiting.

Biological Engineering (20).insiderTip: MIT BE is one of the best in world. Strong outcomes to biotech, pharmaceuticals, healthcare innovation companies.

Massachusetts Institute of Technology.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus independent activities period)
Massachusetts Institute of Technology.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/no pass option for electives only (not core courses)
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Rigorous GIRs: all MIT students complete extensive science and math core regardless of major
UROP (Undergraduate Research Opportunities Program): nearly 90% of students participate, paid research positions
Independent Activities Period (IAP): January month for independent projects, startup launches, exploration
Problem-based learning: emphasis on solving real problems, not just theory
Hands-on maker culture: access to makerspaces, labs, and engineering equipment
Massachusetts Institute of Technology.researchOpportunities: UROP, Summer Research Program, 30 laboratories and centers including Broad Institute, Media Lab, Lincoln Laboratory, interdisciplinary research initiatives
Massachusetts Institute of Technology.studyAbroad: ~12% study abroad. Programs in Beijing, Berlin, Cambridge, Dubai, Ghana, Japan, Switzerland, India.
notableFaculty: Regina Barzilay (Computer Science, Machine Learning for Science)

coreRequirements.description: Extremely rigorous core curriculum required of all Caltech students. Every student, regardless of major, takes the same core courses in first two years: rigorous math, physics, chemistry, biology, engineering, and humanities.

Physics.seniorThesis: Senior thesis or independent research project required
Physics.typicalTimeline: Core courses through freshman/sophomore, specialization junior/senior
Physics.insiderTip: Caltech Physics is world-class. Access to observatories, particle accelerators, LIGO gravitational wave detector. Many students go to Ivy graduate schools.

Engineering.insiderTip: Caltech Engineering is elite but small. Close mentorship. Strong outcomes to tech companies, aerospace, biotech.

Chemistry.typicalTimeline: Core through sophomore, advanced courses junior+
Chemistry.insiderTip: Caltech Chemistry is rigorous and research-focused. Strong outcomes to pharmaceuticals, materials companies, graduate school.

Biological Engineering.insiderTip: Caltech's BE program is among the best in world. Many students pursue medical school or biotech. Strong research publication record.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Caltech CS is small (30-40 undergrads per class) and theory-focused. Strong outcomes to Google, tech companies, graduate school.

California Institute of Technology.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus summer)
California Institute of Technology.averageCourseLoad: 3-5 courses per semester (very rigorous, each course is time-intensive)
California Institute of Technology.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/no pass option limited
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Identical core for all students first two years: ensures all students have same foundation
Residential college system (like Oxbridge): students live in houses with built-in communities
Honor code: students take exams without proctoring, no need to lock bags
Small size: only 1,000 undergrads, 10:1 student-to-faculty ratio
Research-focused: undergraduates involved in cutting-edge research from day one
California Institute of Technology.researchOpportunities: SURF (Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowship), 300+ faculty research groups, JPL (NASA Jet Propulsion Lab) partnerships, LIGO gravitational wave detector, telescopes, particle accelerators
California Institute of Technology.studyAbroad: ~5% study abroad. Limited programs but strong domestic research focus.

coreRequirements.description: Trinity College requires Curriculum 2000: breadth requirements across five areas plus writing and foreign language.

Electrical & Computer Engineering.insiderTip: Duke ECE is strong and collaborative. Direct connections to Research Triangle tech companies and pharma.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Duke CS is growing and strong. Good outcomes to tech companies and graduate school. Research Triangle location is an asset.

Biology.insiderTip: Duke's location near Duke Medical School and numerous research hospitals creates strong pre-med pipeline. Many undergraduates do research in labs.

Chemistry.insiderTip: Duke Chemistry has strong pharmaceutical industry connections. Many students intern at nearby pharma and biotech companies.

Economics.insiderTip: Duke Econ is well-regarded. Strong outcomes to finance, consulting, tech, and graduate school.

Duke University.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus summer)
Duke University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/fail option for electives
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Residential college system: students live in one of 10 residential colleges
Research Triangle location: proximity to Google, Apple, Cisco, IBM, pharma companies
Pratt School of Engineering: excellent engineering programs with strong industry connections
Duke Medical School & Hospital: research opportunities for undergraduates
DukeBox internship program: structured internship placements
Duke University.researchOpportunities: Summer Research, Duke Research Initiative, 200+ research centers, Duke Hospital research programs, collaboration with Microsoft Research, Google, IBM
Duke University.studyAbroad: ~40% study abroad. Programs in London, Berlin, Paris, Geneva, Beijing, India, Ecuador, South Africa.

coreRequirements.description: Unique and rigorous Common Core Curriculum required of all students. Focus on Great Books, intellectual inquiry, and close reading across disciplines.
coreRequirements.writingRequirement: Core writing courses; emphasis on analytical writing

Computer Science.insiderTip: UChicago CS is rigorous and theoretical. Strong outcomes to tech companies and graduate school. Good outcomes to finance careers.

Economics.insiderTip: UChicago Economics is legendary. Chicago School of Economics (Milton Friedman, Gary Becker) tradition. Strong outcomes to finance, policy, academia.

Physics.typicalTimeline: Intro sequence through sophomore, advanced junior+
Physics.insiderTip: UChicago Physics is elite. Fermi Lab collaborations. Access to Argonne National Laboratory. Strong tradition in particle physics.

Chemistry.insiderTip: UChicago Chemistry is rigorous. Strong research culture. Many students pursue pharmaceutical and chemical industry careers.

Biology.insiderTip: UChicago Biology is strong. Good pipeline to medical school and biomedical research. Chicago connections to major hospitals.

University of Chicago.academicCalendar: Quarter system (Fall, Winter, Spring, Summer). Each quarter is ~10 weeks.
University of Chicago.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/no pass option available
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Common Core Curriculum: unique great books curriculum required of all students
Quarter system: four 10-week terms allow flexibility and breadth
Intellectual culture: strong emphasis on inquiry and debate
Chicago School of Economics: famous economic tradition
Dialogue-based learning: many courses emphasize class discussion
University of Chicago.researchOpportunities: UChicago Research Initiative, summer fellowship, 200+ research labs, Fermi Lab access, Argonne National Laboratory partnerships, hospital research programs
University of Chicago.studyAbroad: ~30% study abroad. Programs in London, Paris, Berlin, Beijing, Buenos Aires, Istanbul, Mumbai.

coreRequirements.description: Northwestern schools vary: School of Engineering has engineering requirements; School of Communication has communication focus. Weinberg College of Arts & Sciences has distribution requirements.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Northwestern CS is strong and growing. Close to Chicago tech scene. Good outcomes to Google, Microsoft, trading firms.

Engineering.insiderTip: Northwestern Engineering is rigorous and project-based. Strong outcomes to tech companies, consulting, and graduate school.

Economics.typicalTimeline: Intro courses first year, intermediate sophomore
Economics.insiderTip: Northwestern Econ is well-regarded. Good outcomes to finance, consulting, tech. Kellogg School of Management connections.

Physics.insiderTip: Northwestern Physics is strong. Access to CERN collaborations. Good outcomes to tech and academic research.

Chemistry.insiderTip: Northwestern Chemistry is rigorous. Strong pharmaceutical and materials company recruiting.

Northwestern University.academicCalendar: Quarter system (Fall, Winter, Spring, Summer). Each quarter is ~10 weeks.
Northwestern University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/no pass for electives
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Multiple schools with distinct characters: Weinberg Arts & Sciences, McCormick Engineering, School of Communication
Quarter system: allows flexibility and exploration
Chicago proximity: access to tech companies, consulting firms, financial institutions
Integrated research: undergraduates involved in research from first year
Peer mentoring: strong peer-led learning environment
Northwestern University.researchOpportunities: Summer Research Grant Program, 300+ research labs, undergraduate research partnerships, Chicago connections
Northwestern University.studyAbroad: ~30% study abroad. Programs in London, Paris, Barcelona, Berlin, Beijing, Buenos Aires, Dubai.

coreRequirements.description: Engineering program has structured requirements. Arts & Sciences has distribution-based requirements. All students must complete breadth requirements across areas.

Biomedical Engineering.insiderTip: Johns Hopkins BME is the best in the nation. Direct access to Johns Hopkins Hospital and research. Many students go to top medical schools.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Johns Hopkins CS is strong and growing. Good outcomes to tech companies and graduate school. Strong connections to Baltimore tech scene.

Engineering.insiderTip: Johns Hopkins Engineering is top-tier. Strong outcomes to tech companies, defense contracting, and graduate school.

Physics.insiderTip: Johns Hopkins Physics is well-regarded. Access to Space Telescope Science Institute. Good outcomes to academia and research labs.

Chemistry.insiderTip: Johns Hopkins Chemistry has strong pharmaceutical connections. Many students go to pharma or biotech companies.

Johns Hopkins University.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus summer)
Johns Hopkins University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/fail option for electives
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Johns Hopkins Hospital & Medical School: unparalleled research opportunities
Applied Physics Laboratory: major government research facility partnerships
Biomedical Engineering: strongest program in the nation
Research focus: undergraduates involved in cutting-edge research
Small class sizes: emphasis on undergraduate instruction
Johns Hopkins University.researchOpportunities: CURE (Center for Undergraduate Research Experiences), Summer Research Fellowship, 300+ research labs, Johns Hopkins Hospital research, Space Telescope Science Institute access
Johns Hopkins University.studyAbroad: ~25% study abroad. Programs in London, Berlin, Beijing, Buenos Aires, India, South Africa.

coreRequirements.description: Rice has distribution requirements but strong emphasis on self-design. Students choose courses across multiple disciplines to gain breadth.

Engineering.insiderTip: Rice Engineering has strong spirit of innovation. Students build things from day one. Excellent outcomes to tech, aerospace, energy sectors.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Rice CS is strong and has great student culture. Good outcomes to tech companies and consulting firms.

Physics.insiderTip: Rice Physics is small and collegial. Good outcomes to graduate school and research careers.

Chemistry.insiderTip: Rice Chemistry is research-focused. Access to specialized facilities. Good outcomes to pharma and chemical companies.

Economics.insiderTip: Rice Econ is well-regarded and small. Great relationship with Houston business community. Strong outcomes to finance and consulting.

Rice University.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus summer)
Rice University.gradingSystem: Letter grades (A-F), pass/no pass option for non-major courses
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Residential college system (8 colleges): tight-knit living learning communities
Small size: only 4,000 undergraduates, 6:1 student-to-faculty ratio
No core curriculum: students design own education with guidance
Exceptional funding: full need-based financial aid, no loans
Honor code: direct academic integrity culture
Rice University.researchOpportunities: Rice Summer Research Program, Undergraduate Research Assistantship, 150+ research centers, engineering design competitions
Rice University.studyAbroad: ~25% study abroad. Programs in London, Berlin, Paris, Buenos Aires, Beijing, and more.

coreRequirements.description: Vanderbilt requires breadth requirements across schools. College of Arts & Sciences has distribution system; Engineering and Owen Business School have specific requirements.

Engineering.insiderTip: Vanderbilt Engineering is growing and well-regarded. Located in Nashville tech hub. Good outcomes to energy, healthcare tech, and consulting companies.

Computer Science.insiderTip: Vanderbilt CS is strong. Nashville location with growing tech scene. Good outcomes to tech companies and consulting.

Economics.insiderTip: Vanderbilt Econ is well-regarded. Strong outcomes to finance, consulting, and graduate school. Connections to Nashville financial institutions.

Chemistry.insiderTip: Vanderbilt Chemistry has strong research culture. Good outcomes to pharma and chemical companies.

Physics.insiderTip: Vanderbilt Physics is solid. Good outcomes to tech and graduate school.

Vanderbilt University.academicCalendar: Semester (Fall: September-December, Spring: January-May, plus summer)
uniqueAcademicFeatures: Strong financial aid: meets full demonstrated need
Nashville location: growing tech and healthcare hub
Residential colleges: living learning communities
Vanderbilt Medical Center: research opportunities
Owen Graduate Business School: undergraduate business-minded connections
Vanderbilt University.researchOpportunities: Summer Research Program, UROP-style undergraduate research, 300+ research labs, Nashville healthcare and biotech partnerships
Vanderbilt University.studyAbroad: ~30% study abroad. Programs in London, Paris, Berlin, Beijing, Buenos Aires, Spain.

coreRequirements.description: Varies by school. College of Arts & Sciences has distribution requirements. School of Engineering has engineering core. Both have emphasis on breadth.

Engineering.insiderTip: WashU Engineering is excellent. Strong outcomes to tech, aerospace, healthcare. St. Louis biotech connections.
